label: Audience
description: The intended readership or age group for which the work is primarily designed.

tags.definition: Designed for children aged 0–4; typically picture books, board books, or read-aloud material
Designed for early and middle childhood readers, approximately ages 5–8; early chapter books and illustrated stories
Designed for readers approximately ages 8–12; chapter books with more complex plots and themes
Designed for readers approximately ages 13–18; may include mature themes while stopping short of adult content
Broad catch-all for under-18 content when a more specific age tier is unavailable; maps to legacy OL 'Juvenile fiction' / 'Juvenile literature'
Designed for general adult readership; no upper age restriction implied
Designed for scholarly or research contexts; assumes significant background knowledge in the subject
Designed for practitioners in a specific field; assumes professional training or expertise
Intentionally accessible across age groups; not targeted at a specific demographic
